query: our renderer hand-writes a barrier before and after every pass and it breaks every time we reorder passes — I want passes to just declare what they read and write and have ordering and transitions figured out for us
rationale: direct: render/frame graph deriving order + barriers from declared reads/writes

query: we have a g-buffer, an SSAO target, and a bloom mip chain all alive at once and VRAM is tight — can we reuse the same memory for targets that aren't used at the same time
rationale: implicit: transient render-target aliasing by disjoint lifetime, no Vulkan named

query: every time we use a new material the frame hitches for a few ms; I think it's compiling the graphics state on first draw — how do we get rid of the stall
rationale: implicit: precompiled + cached pipeline state objects, warm the PSO cache

query: we're calling the allocator once per texture and once per mesh and it falls over on big levels — restructure how we hand out GPU memory
rationale: device-memory: sub-allocate from few large blocks, allocation-count cap

query: the CPU and GPU keep stalling on each other each frame; we have one set of uniform buffers and one command pool shared across frames — fix the frame pacing
rationale: frames-in-flight: per-frame resource sets + fence so CPU doesn't outrun GPU

query: we want one big array of all our textures that the shader indexes by an integer instead of binding a descriptor set per draw
rationale: bindless / update-after-bind descriptor arrays indexed by handle

query: how do I get a sampled read of a render target right after I wrote to it as a color attachment — what stage and access masks and layout transition do I need
rationale: synchronization: pipeline barrier masks + image-layout transition, read-after-write

query: we have 40 feature combinations for our lit shader and build times exploded; should we compile a variant per combo or branch in one ubershader, and how do we recompile on save without restarting
rationale: shader-system: permutations vs ubershader + spec constants, hot-reload

query: set up a three.js scene with orbit controls and a glTF model and some postprocessing passes
rationale: near-miss: high-level web 3D -> threejs-guide, no explicit-API resource/sync concerns

query: help me get the BRDF math right in my fragment shader — the specular highlight looks wrong, it's all GLSL, no pipeline or resource changes
rationale: near-miss: pure shading-language math, no pipeline/resource/sync concern

query: restructure this CPU particle array from AoS to SoA so the update loop stops missing cache
upload a static vertex buffer to fast GPU-only memory — I think I need a staging buffer and a copy, then a barrier before the draw reads it
rationale: validation positive: staging upload to device-local + transfer->read barrier

query: I want to record draw calls from several worker threads at once and then submit them in order — how should I lay out command pools and buffers
rationale: validation positive: per-thread pools, primary/secondary, multi-threaded recording

query: replace our pile of binary semaphores between the graphics and async-compute queues with a single monotonic counter we can also wait on from the CPU
rationale: validation positive: timeline semaphores for queue->queue + CPU waits

query: tune my WebGL draw-call batching and instancing in the browser to keep the framerate up
rationale: validation negative: high-level web 3D, no explicit-API device/memory/sync ownership
